title: The Silent Web Server
problem: Users report the website is down. Connection refused on port 80.

investigations.systemctl status nginx:
● nginx.service - A high performance web server
   Active: inactive (dead)
investigations.curl localhost: curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 80: Connection refused

resolution: Nginx service restored. Website is back online.
hints: Check if nginx is running with systemctl status.
Try starting the service.

title: Permission Denied Crisis
problem: Developers cannot update the website. 'Permission denied' when writing to /var/www/html.
resolution: Ownership and permissions adjusted. Write access restored.
hints: Check directory ownership with ls -ld.
Use chown or chmod to fix access.

title: Disk Full Disaster
problem: The system is sluggish and logs are failing. Monitoring shows /dev/sda1 at 100%.

investigations.df -h:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        20G   20G     0 100% /

resolution: Large log file cleared. Disk space reclaimed.
hints: Use df -h to confirm disk usage.
Find the culprit in /var/log using du -sh.

title: Provision New Developer
problem: New hire 'sarah' needs a user account with sudo access and a home directory.
fix_commands: sudo useradd -m sarah && sudo usermod -aG sudo sarah
resolution: User 'sarah' created and added to the sudo group.
hints: Use useradd or adduser to create the account.
Use usermod to add her to the sudo group.

title: Configure Log Archival
problem: The /var/www/html/backup.tar.gz file needs to be moved to the /mnt/storage/backups directory.
resolution: Backup file moved successfully to long-term storage.
hints: Use the 'mv' command to move the file.
Confirm the destination directory exists.

title: Patch System Security
problem: Weekly maintenance: Update the package list and upgrade only the 'openssh-server' package.
fix_commands: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade openssh-server
resolution: OpenSSH server updated to the latest security patch.
hints: Always run 'apt update' first.
Use '--only-upgrade' to target a specific package.

title: Website Down: Multi-Stage Recovery
problem: The website is showing a '500 Internal Server Error'. Nginx is running, but something is wrong behind the scenes.

investigations.tail /var/log/nginx/error.log: connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream: http://127.0.0.1:3306

resolution: Nginx was OK, but the Backend Database was down. MySQL started.
hints: Check Nginx error logs for clues.
If it's a 500 error, check backend services like database or app server.

title: Website Not Loading (Firewall Issue)
problem: A user reports that your website is not loading in the browser. Nginx is running, but the site is unreachable from outside.

investigations.ufw status:
Status: active
To Action From
-- ------ ----
22 ALLOW Anywhere

resolution: Port 80 (HTTP) was blocked by the firewall. Access restored!
hints: If it works on localhost but not from outside, check the firewall.
Use 'ufw status' to see active rules.

title: Domain Name Resolution Failure
problem: You cannot reach external websites by name (e.g., google.com), but you can reach them by IP address (e.g., 8.8.8.8).

investigations.ping 8.8.8.8: 64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=117 time=12.4 ms
investigations.ping google.com: ping: google.com: Name or service not known

resolution: DNS configuration corrected. Name resolution restored.
hints: Check your DNS settings in /etc/resolv.conf.
Try adding a reliable nameserver like 8.8.8.8.

title: Cloud Load Balancer: Health Check Failing
problem: The cloud load balancer is reporting 'Unhealthy' for all backend nodes. The app is running, but no traffic is reaching it.

investigations.ufw status:
Status: active
To Action From
-- ------ ----
3000 DENY Anywhere

resolution: The firewall was blocking the Load Balancer's health check port (3000). Rule added.
hints: If the app is running but the LB says it's down, check the port used for health checks.
Ensure the firewall allows traffic from the LB's range (or just the port).

title: VPC Peering: Connectivity Lost
problem: Server A in VPC-1 cannot reach Database B in VPC-2. They were working yesterday. Peering is active.

investigations.ip route:
default via 10.0.1.1 dev eth0
10.0.1.0/24 dev eth0 proto kernel ...
investigations.ping 10.0.2.55:
PING 10.0.2.55 (10.0.2.55): 56 data bytes
From 10.0.1.1: icmp_seq=1 Destination Net Unreachable

resolution: Static route to the peered VPC was missing from the local routing table.
hints: Check the routing table with 'ip route'.
Ensure there is a route for the 10.0.2.0/24 subnet.

title: Slow Downloads: MTU Mismatch
problem: Large file transfers are extremely slow and frequently time out, but small pings work fine. This started after moving to a new cloud provider.

investigations.ping -s 1472 -M do 8.8.8.8:
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 1472(1500) bytes of data.
From 10.0.1.1 icmp_seq=1 Frag needed and DF set (mtu = 1500)

fix_commands: sudo ip link set eth0 mtu 1500
resolution: MTU was set to 9000 (Jumbo Frames), which the external network didn't support. Reset to 1500.
hints: Large packets failing while small ones pass often points to MTU issues.
Check MTU with 'ip link show'.

title: DNS CNAME Loop Detected
problem: The domain 'api.shellcraft.io' is failing to resolve. Browsers show 'Too many redirects' or resolution timeout.

investigations.dig api.shellcraft.io:
;; ANSWER SECTION:
api.shellcraft.io. 60 IN CNAME web.shellcraft.io.
web.shellcraft.io. 60 IN CNAME api.shellcraft.io.

resolution: Identified a CNAME loop where A pointed to B and B pointed back to A. Fixed record to point to an A record.
hints: Use 'dig' to inspect the CNAME chain.
Look for records that point to each other.

title: SSL/TLS Port 443 Timeout
problem: The site works via HTTP (80) but fails completely on HTTPS (443). Nginx says it's listening on both.

investigations.ufw status:
Status: active
To Action From
-- ------ ----
80 ALLOW Anywhere
22 ALLOW Anywhere

resolution: Port 443 was not allowed in the firewall. HTTPS traffic restored.
hints: Check if the port is open in the firewall with 'ufw status'.
Confirm the service is actually listening on 443.

title: VPC Peering: Overlapping CIDR
problem: You are trying to peer VPC-A (10.0.0.0/16) with VPC-B (10.0.0.0/24), but the request is failing immediately.
resolution: VPC Peering cannot be established between VPCs with overlapping CIDR blocks.
hints: VPC peering requires non-overlapping IP ranges.
Check the CIDR blocks of both VPCs.

title: Transit Gateway: Missing Propagation
problem: VPC-C is attached to the Transit Gateway (TGW), but it cannot reach VPC-D which is also attached. Routing looks correct on the VPC side.

investigations.aws ec2 describe-transit-gateway-route-t: Table-1: No routes for VPC-C range 10.3.0.0/16

resolution: The TGW attachment was not propagating routes to the Transit Gateway route table.
hints: Check Transit Gateway route table propagations.
Ensure the attachment is associated and propagating.

title: Security Group vs NACL Conflict
problem: SSH is allowed in the Security Group, but you still can't connect. You checked the firewall on the OS, and it's disabled.
fix_commands: aws ec2 replace-network-acl-entry --network-acl-id acl-123 --rule-number 100 --protocol tcp --port-range From=22,To=22 --cidr-block 0.0.0.0/0 --rule-action allow
resolution: The Network ACL (NACL) was explicitly denying port 22, overriding the Security Group allow rule.
hints: NACLs are evaluated before Security Groups for inbound traffic.
Check if a 'Deny' rule exists in your Subnet's NACL.

title: S3 Endpoint: DNS Resolution Issue
problem: An EC2 instance in a private subnet cannot reach S3, even though an S3 VPC Endpoint is created. You want to avoid using the public internet.
resolution: Private DNS was not enabled on the VPC Endpoint, causing the instance to still try and reach S3 via its public IP.
hints: VPC Endpoints often require 'Private DNS' to be enabled to override public resolution.
Check your VPC Endpoint configuration.

title: Global Accelerator: High Latency
problem: Users in Asia are reporting high latency to your US-based application, even though you enabled AWS Global Accelerator.
fix_commands: echo 'Wait for Global Accelerator deployment to complete' > /tmp/wait.txt
resolution: Global Accelerator was still in the 'IN_PROGRESS' state, meaning the global Anycast nodes were not yet fully optimized.
hints: Global Accelerator takes time to propagate across all edge locations.
Check the deployment status of your accelerator.

title: Zombie Container
problem: A container named 'web-app' is unresponsive and blocking port 80. It needs to be forcefully removed.

investigations.docker ps:
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND   CREATED         STATUS         PORTS                NAMES
1a2b3c4d5e6f   nginx     "..."     5 minutes ago   Up 5 minutes   0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp   web-app

fix_commands: docker kill web-app && docker rm web-app
docker stop web-app && docker rm web-app
resolution: The unresponsive container was forcefully removed, freeing up the port.
hints: Use 'docker ps' to find the container.
Try 'docker rm -f' to force removal if it's stuck.

title: Image Bloat
problem: The disk is filling up with unused Docker images. You need to clean up all dangling images.

investigations.docker images:
REPOSITORY   TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED       SIZE
<none>       <none>    7f8e9d0c1b2a   2 days ago    500MB
<none>       <none>    1a2b3c4d5e6f   5 days ago    1.2GB

resolution: Dangling images were removed, reclaiming several gigabytes of disk space.
hints: Look for images with <none> tags.
The 'prune' command is very useful for cleanup.

title: Launch a Database
problem: Start a MariaDB container named 'db-server' in the background, using the 'mariadb:latest' image, and set the root password to 'secret123' using environment variables.
resolution: MariaDB container is now running in the background with the specified configuration.
hints: Use '-d' for detached mode and '--name' to name it.
Use '-e' to set environment variables.

title: Unhealthy Healthcheck
problem: The 'api-server' container is running but marked as 'unhealthy' in Docker, causing the load balancer to skip it.

investigations.docker ps:
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE        STATUS                     NAMES
abc123def456   node-api     Up 10 minutes (unhealthy)  api-server
investigations.docker inspect --format='{{json .State.H: {"Status":"unhealthy","FailingStreak":5,"Log":[{"Output":"curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8080: Connection refused\n"}]}

fix_commands: docker run -d --name api-server -p 8080:8080 --health-cmd 'curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1' node-api
resolution: Corrected the healthcheck command port and restarted the container.
hints: Check 'docker inspect' to see healthcheck logs.
Ensure the healthcheck command points to the correct internal port.

title: Isolate the Network
problem: The 'web' container cannot reach the 'database' container by its name, even though both are running.

investigations.docker network ls:
NETWORK ID     NAME      DRIVER    SCOPE
1234567890ab   bridge    bridge    local
investigations.docker exec web ping database: ping: database: Name or service not known

fix_commands: docker network create app-net && docker network connect app-net web && docker network connect app-net database
resolution: Containers were on the default bridge network where DNS resolution by name is not supported. Moved them to a user-defined bridge network.
hints: Default bridge network doesn't support service discovery via DNS.
Use user-defined networks for container-to-container communication.

title: The Rootless Volume Trap
problem: The 'app' container (running as user 'node') fails to write logs to a mounted host directory /opt/logs.
resolution: The host directory was owned by root, but the container process was running as a non-root user (UID 1000). Adjusted ownership on the host.
hints: Check the UID of the user inside the container.
Ensure host permissions align with the container user.

title: Scaling Collision
problem: Attempting to scale the 'worker' service to 3 replicas using Docker Compose fails with a port conflict.

investigations.docker-compose up --scale worker=3: ERROR: for worker_2  Cannot start service worker: driver failed programming external connectivity on endpoint... Bind for 0.0.0.0:8080 failed: port is already allocated

resolution: Removed the static host port binding in docker-compose.yml to allow Docker to assign dynamic ports for multiple replicas.
hints: You cannot bind multiple containers to the same host port.
Use dynamic port mapping when scaling.

title: Tame the Logs
problem: Configure the Docker daemon to limit log file size to 10MB and keep only 3 rotations to prevent disk exhaustion.
resolution: Docker logging policy updated globally with size limits and rotation.
hints: Modify /etc/docker/daemon.json.
Don't forget to restart the Docker service.

title: CPU Throttling Mystery
problem: The 'processor' container is running much slower than expected. You suspect it's being throttled.

investigations.docker stats --no-stream:
CONTAINER ID   NAME        CPU %     MEM USAGE / LIMIT
xyz789       processor   49.98%    100MiB / 2GiB

resolution: The container had a strict CPU quota of 0.5 cores. Increased the limit to 2.0 cores.
hints: Check 'docker stats' for current usage.
Use 'docker update' to change limits on the fly.

title: The OOM Reaper Strikes
problem: A memory-intensive Java application container keeps restarting every few minutes.

investigations.dmesg | grep -i oom: Out of memory: Kill process 12345 (java) score 950

resolution: The container was exceeding its 512MB memory limit and getting killed by the kernel OOM killer. Increased memory limit to 2GB.
hints: Check if OOMKilled is true in container state.
Look at system logs with dmesg.

title: Ghost in the Multi-Stage
problem: The multi-stage build finishes successfully, but the final image fails to start because 'app.jar' is missing.

investigations.docker run my-app: docker: Error response from daemon: OCI runtime create failed: ... no such file or directory
investigations.cat Dockerfile:
FROM maven AS build
RUN mvn package
FROM openjdk:jre-slim
COPY --from=build /target/app.jar /app.jar

fix_commands: Update COPY path in Dockerfile to match the actual build artifact location.
resolution: The COPY command in the final stage used a wrong source path from the build stage. Corrected the path.
hints: Verify the working directory in the build stage.
Double-check the path of generated artifacts.

title: Registry Access Denied
problem: Failed to push image to a private registry. Error: 'insufficient_scope: authorization failed'.

investigations.docker push my-registry.com/my-app:
The push refers to repository [my-registry.com/my-app]
error parsing HTTP 401 response body: unexpected end of JSON input

resolution: The user was not authenticated with the private registry. Logged in using 'docker login'.
hints: Check if you are logged in to the registry.
Verify credentials in ~/.docker/config.json.

title: Swarm Service Stagnation
problem: Docker Swarm service 'web' shows 0/3 replicas running. Tasks are stuck in 'Pending' state.

investigations.docker service ps web:
ID   NAME    IMAGE   NODE   DESIRED STATE   CURRENT STATE    ERROR
123  web.1   nginx          Running         Pending          "no suitable node (insufficient resources on 3 nodes)"
investigations.docker node ls:
ID    HOSTNAME   STATUS    AVAILABILITY   MANAGER STATUS
abc   node-1     Ready     Active         Leader

resolution: The service had memory limits higher than available on any single node. Lowered memory limits to allow scheduling.
hints: Check 'docker service ps' for error messages.
Verify node resources vs service requirements.

title: Port Already in Use
problem: Cannot start a new Nginx container on port 80 because another process is already using it.

investigations.docker run -p 80:80 nginx: Error response from daemon: driver failed programming external connectivity on endpoint... Bind for 0.0.0.0:80 failed: port is already allocated

resolution: Identified that Apache was running on the host port 80. Stopped Apache or used a different host port.
hints: Use 'ss' or 'netstat' to find what's using the port.
Change the host-side port mapping if needed.

title: Wrong Alias Discovery
problem: A frontend container cannot connect to the backend container using the alias 'api-service', even though it's configured in the network.

investigations.docker exec frontend curl http://api-ser: curl: (6) Could not resolve host: api-service

fix_commands: docker network connect --alias api-service my-net backend
Update frontend to use 'api-server' instead of 'api-service'
resolution: The backend container had 'api-server' as an alias, but the frontend was trying to use 'api-service'. Corrected the alias or the frontend config.
hints: Check network aliases in 'docker inspect'.
Verify the service name used in the application config.

title: The Vanishing Data
problem: When the 'db' container is recreated, all data is lost. You realize it was using an anonymous volume.
resolution: Replaced the anonymous volume with a named volume 'mysql-data' to ensure persistence across container removals.
hints: Anonymous volumes have random hex names.
Always use named volumes for production data.

title: Survive the Reboot
problem: Ensure the 'monitoring' container always starts automatically when the Docker daemon or the host machine reboots.
resolution: Updated the container's restart policy to 'always'.
hints: The default restart policy is 'no'.
Use 'docker update' for existing containers.

title: Entrypoint Overload
problem: Running 'docker run my-app bash' doesn't open a shell but instead tries to pass 'bash' as an argument to the main application, which then crashes.

investigations.docker logs my-app: Error: Could not find or load main class bash

fix_commands: Change ENTRYPOINT to CMD in Dockerfile if you want it to be easily overridable.
resolution: The Dockerfile used the exec form of ENTRYPOINT, which treats arguments as parameters to the command. Used '--entrypoint' to override it.
hints: ENTRYPOINT is not easily overridden by trailing arguments.
CMD is easily overridden by trailing arguments.

title: Optimizing the Build Cache
problem: The Docker build for a Node.js app takes too long because it runs 'npm install' every time a source file changes.
fix_commands: Update Dockerfile to COPY package.json first.
resolution: Reordered Dockerfile layers to copy package files and run npm install before copying the rest of the source code, leveraging the build cache.
hints: Only copy files needed for the current step.
Frequent changes should happen as late as possible in the Dockerfile.

title: Secret Clearance
problem: Deploy a service that needs a database password. Use Docker Swarm secrets instead of environment variables for security.
fix_commands: echo "mypassword" | docker secret create db_pass - && docker service create --name web --secret db_pass nginx
resolution: Created a Swarm secret and attached it to the service. The secret is now available at /run/secrets/db_pass in the container.
hints: Secrets are only available in Swarm mode.
Secrets are mounted as files by default.

title: Uninitialized Project
problem: You have a new project directory 'website' but it's not a Git repository. Initialize it.

investigations.git status: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git

resolution: Repository initialized. You can now start tracking changes.
hints: Use the command to start a new Git repository.
Check 'git help init' if unsure.

title: First Commit Flow
problem: You've initialized the repo. Now track README.md and make your first commit.

investigations.git status:
On branch main
Untracked files:
  README.md

fix_commands: git add README.md && git commit -m 'Initial commit'
resolution: Files staged and committed successfully.
hints: You need to 'add' before you 'commit'.
Don't forget the commit message with -m.

title: Branching Out
problem: Create a new branch named 'develop' and switch to it.
fix_commands: git branch develop && git checkout develop
resolution: New branch created and active.
hints: You can do this in two steps or one with a shortcut flag.
Check 'git branch' to see your branches.

title: The Ghost Push
problem: You're trying to push to 'origin' but Git says the remote doesn't exist.

investigations.git push origin main: fatal: 'origin' does not appear to be a git repository

resolution: Remote 'origin' added. You can now push your code.
hints: Check your configured remotes with 'git remote -v'.
Use 'git remote add' to connect to a server.

title: Diverged History
problem: Your push was rejected because the remote contains work that you do not have locally.

investigations.git push origin main:
error: failed to push some refs to ...
hint: Updates were rejected because the remote contains work that you do not have locally.

fix_commands: git fetch origin && git merge origin/main
resolution: Remote changes integrated. You can now push your work.
hints: You need to bring the remote changes into your local branch first.
Use 'git pull'.

title: Emergency Stash
problem: You're in the middle of a feature but need to switch to 'main' to fix a bug. Stash your changes.

investigations.git status:
On branch feature
Changes not staged for commit:
  modified:   app.js

resolution: Work-in-progress stashed safely. Working directory is clean.
hints: Use the command to save temporary changes without committing.
Check 'git stash --help'.

title: Recovering Stashed Work
problem: You've finished the bug fix on 'main'. Switch back to 'feature' and restore your stashed work.
fix_commands: git checkout feature && git stash pop
resolution: Returned to feature branch and popped stash. Ready to resume work.
hints: Switch branches first.
Use 'pop' to apply and remove from stash list.

title: Accidental Stage
problem: You accidentally staged 'secrets.env'. Unstage it but keep the file.

investigations.git status:
Changes to be committed:
  new file:   secrets.env

resolution: File unstaged. It is now untracked.
hints: Use 'reset' or 'rm --cached' to remove from index.
Don't use -f as it might delete the file.

title: Oops, Wrong Message
problem: You just committed with the message 'stuff'. Change it to 'feat: add user authentication'.
resolution: Commit message updated successfully.
hints: Use the 'amend' flag.
This only works for the most recent commit.

title: Cleaning Up Branches
problem: The branch 'legacy-v1' is no longer needed. Delete it.
resolution: Branch 'legacy-v1' deleted.
hints: Use the branch command with the delete flag.
Standard delete is -d.

title: Release Tagging
problem: The current state is ready for release. Create an annotated tag 'v1.0.0'.

investigations.git status: nothing to commit, working tree clean

resolution: Tag 'v1.0.0' created.
hints: Use 'git tag -a' for an annotated tag.
Include a message with -m.

title: Cherry-Pick a Fix
problem: An important fix was committed to 'develop' (hash: a1b2c3d). Apply it to your current 'hotfix' branch.

investigations.git log develop -n 1:
commit a1b2c3d...
Author: ...

    fix: patch security hole

resolution: Commit a1b2c3d applied to hotfix branch.
hints: Use the command to pick a specific commit by hash.
Check 'git help cherry-pick'.

title: Untracked Clutter
problem: Your workspace is full of temporary .tmp files. Remove all untracked files.
resolution: Untracked files removed.
hints: Use the 'clean' command.
Use -f to force the removal.

title: Renaming for Clarity
problem: The current branch 'fix1' is too vague. Rename it to 'fix/login-loop'.
resolution: Branch renamed to 'fix/login-loop'.
hints: The -m flag stands for 'move' or rename.
No need to specify the old name if you're on it.

title: Bad Commit in History
problem: Commit '5e6f7g8' introduced a bug that broke production. Revert it safely.
resolution: Revert commit created. Production fix in progress.
hints: 'revert' creates a new commit that undoes the changes.
Don't use 'reset' as it rewrites history.

title: Searching the Code
problem: Find every occurrence of 'TODO' in the tracked files of your repository.
resolution: Search results displayed.
hints: Git has its own version of 'grep'.
It only searches tracked files.

title: The Nuclear Option
problem: Everything is a mess. Discard ALL local changes (tracked and untracked) to match the last commit.

investigations.git status:
On branch main
Changes not staged for commit:
  modified:   app.js
Untracked files:
  trash.txt

resolution: Working directory reset to HEAD. Clutter removed.
hints: Use 'reset --hard' for tracked files.
Use 'clean -fd' for untracked files and directories.

title: Pushing Tags
problem: You've created tag 'v2.0'. Push it to 'origin'.
resolution: Tag 'v2.0' pushed to remote.
hints: Specify the remote and the tag name.
Normal 'git push' doesn't send tags.

title: Checking Out a Commit
problem: You need to see the state of the project at commit '1a2b3c4'. Go there in a detached HEAD state.
resolution: Switched to commit 1a2b3c4. HEAD is now detached.
hints: You can checkout hashes just like branches.
Check 'git status' to see your state.

title: Connecting Upstream
problem: You've forked a project. Add the original repo 'https://github.com/original/repo.git' as a remote named 'upstream'.
resolution: Remote 'upstream' added successfully.
hints: Use 'git remote add'.
Specify the name 'upstream' and the URL.

title: The Silent Pipeline
problem: You pushed code to the 'feat/login' branch, but the GitHub Actions workflow didn't trigger.
resolution: Workflow trigger updated to include the feature branch.
hints: Check the 'on' section of your workflow file.
Is the current branch listed in the trigger branches?

title: YAML Indentation Error
problem: The pipeline failed immediately with a YAML syntax error. Fix the indentation in the 'steps' section.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/build.yml:
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Checkout
    uses: actions/checkout@v4

resolution: YAML indentation fixed. The 'uses' keyword is now properly nested under the step.
hints: YAML uses indentation to define structure.
The 'uses' keyword should be at the same level as 'name'.

title: Missing Checkout Step
problem: The 'npm install' step failed because 'package.json' was not found in the workspace.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/ci.yml:
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Install
        run: npm install

resolution: Added 'actions/checkout@v4' step. The code is now available for the runner.
hints: The runner starts with an empty directory.
How do you get your code onto the runner machine?

title: Docker Build Failure
problem: The Docker build step failed. The Dockerfile is missing the 'FROM' instruction.

investigations.git log -n 1:
commit abc123...
Author: ...

    chore: clean up Dockerfile

resolution: Added base image to Dockerfile. Build successful.
hints: Every Dockerfile must start with a base image.
What is the keyword for specifying a base image?

title: Deploy to Staging
problem: Trigger the staging deployment manually using workflow_dispatch.
resolution: Manual trigger (workflow_dispatch) added to the deployment workflow.
hints: Which keyword allows for manual triggers in GitHub Actions?
Add it under the 'on' section.

title: Exposed API Key
problem: A security scan found an API key hardcoded in the pipeline YAML. Move it to a secret.
resolution: API key moved to repository secrets. Security risk mitigated.
hints: Never hardcode secrets in YAML.
Use the secrets context syntax: ${{ secrets.NAME }}

title: Broken Dependencies
problem: The build is failing because it's using an old Node.js version (v12) which is not compatible with the project.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/ci.yml:
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: node -v

resolution: Added 'setup-node' action with version 20. Build dependencies are now compatible.
hints: You can specify the runtime version using a setup action.
Check the marketplace for 'setup-node'.

title: Stale Cache
problem: The build is using a corrupted cache. Clear the cache by changing the cache key.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/ci.yml:
- name: Cache dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: npm-cache-v1

resolution: Cache key updated to v2. This will force a fresh download of dependencies.
hints: GitHub Actions uses the 'key' to find a cache.
Changing the key forces a cache bust.

title: Permission Denied on Script
problem: The 'deploy.sh' step failed with 'Permission denied'.
resolution: Added executable permission to the script before running it.
hints: Scripts must have the 'x' bit set to run in a Linux environment.
Use 'chmod +x'.

title: Wrong Registry Tag
problem: The deployment is pulling the 'latest' image, but the pipeline is pushing with a specific version tag. Fix the deployment command.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
- name: Deploy
        run: kubectl set image deployment/web web=my-app:latest

resolution: Deployment command updated to use the commit SHA tag instead of 'latest'.
hints: Using 'latest' is often unreliable in production.
Use the current commit SHA for a unique, immutable tag.

title: Pipeline Secret Leak
problem: The pipeline logs are showing a raw password because it wasn't masked. Fix the secret reference.
resolution: Changed environment variable reference to a secret reference. GitHub Actions will now automatically mask the output.
hints: Variables in 'env' are not masked by default.
Use the 'secrets' context for sensitive data.

title: Conditional Job
problem: Configure the 'deploy' job to only run if the 'test' job completes successfully.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/main.yml:
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

resolution: Added 'needs: test' to the deploy job. It now waits for the test job to succeed.
hints: Which keyword defines dependencies between jobs?
It should be placed under the job ID.

title: Build Matrix Failure
problem: The build is failing on Node 14 because it's end-of-life. Remove '14' from the build matrix.
resolution: Removed Node 14 from the build matrix. Pipeline is now green on supported versions.
hints: Look for the 'matrix' section under 'strategy'.
Remove the unsupported version from the list.

title: Artifact Path Mismatch
problem: The 'upload-artifact' action is failing because it can't find the 'dist' folder. The build actually produces a 'build' folder.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/ci.yml:
- name: Upload
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: dist

resolution: Updated artifact path to 'build'. Artifacts are now correctly captured.
hints: Compare the 'path' in the YAML with the actual folder name on disk.
Use 'ls' to see the current directory structure.

title: Missing Environment Variable
problem: The integration tests are failing because the 'DATABASE_URL' environment variable is missing.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/test.yml:
- name: Run Tests
        run: npm test

fix_commands: sed -i '/run: npm test/i \        env:\n          DATABASE_URL: mongodb://localhost:27017' .github/workflows/test.yml
resolution: Added 'DATABASE_URL' environment variable to the test step.
hints: Steps can have their own 'env' section.
Add the required variable before the 'run' command.

title: Auto-Assign Reviewers
problem: Add a step to the 'pull_request' workflow that uses the 'actions/github-script' to log the PR number.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/pr.yml:
on: pull_request
jobs:
  info:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

fix_commands:
sed -i '/checkout@v4/a \      - name: Log PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: console.log(context.issue.number)' .github/workflows/pr.yml
resolution: Added github-script step to log PR metadata.
hints: Check the marketplace for 'github-script'.
Use it to interact with the GitHub context.

title: Slow Build Cleanup
problem: The runner is running out of disk space. Add a step to prune Docker system after the build.

investigations.cat .github/workflows/docker.yml:
- name: Build
        run: docker build -t app .

fix_commands: sed -i '/docker build/a \      - name: Cleanup\n        run: docker system prune -f' .github/workflows/docker.yml
resolution: Added Docker cleanup step to reclaim disk space on the runner.
hints: Runners have limited disk space.
How do you remove unused Docker data?

title: Tag Trigger Loop
problem: The pipeline is triggering itself in an infinite loop because it creates a Git tag which then triggers another run. Fix the trigger to ignore tags.
resolution: Removed tag trigger. The pipeline will now only run on branch pushes.
hints: The 'tags' filter in the 'on' section controls tag-based triggers.
Delete the tags filter to break the loop.

title: Concurrency Control
problem: Prevent multiple deployments from running at the same time by adding a concurrency group named 'deploy'.
resolution: Concurrency group added. Subsequent deployments will now cancel previous ones.
hints: Use the 'concurrency' keyword at the top level of the YAML.
It takes a group name as a value.

title: Unsupported Runner
problem: The job is failing because it's trying to run on 'macos-latest' which is not available for this organization. Switch to 'ubuntu-latest'.
resolution: Switched to ubuntu-latest runner. Build now executes on supported infrastructure.
hints: Check the 'runs-on' value.
What is the most common Linux runner in GitHub Actions?
